title: Cancer translocation breakpoints share the recombination hotspot biophysical signature: Fisher OR=2.34 enrichment, biophysics Cohen's d ~ +0.99

subject.display: Migration cohort for 'Cancer translocation breakpoints share the recombination hotspot biophysical sig'
subject.note: Inclusion predicates intentionally empty at migration time; the implicit member set is defined by the v1.1 premises. Re-author with explicit inclusion predicates to tighten the subject.

source.note: Cancer translocation breakpoint coordinates from the PolymerGenomicsAPI breakpoints layer (curated from MITELMAN + cosmic translocation panels).

operations.note: Top quartile by crossover rate (16 binned windows in the original A4 analysis).

statistics.name: median_recomb_percentile_at_breakpoints
statistics.note: Median percentile of crossover rate at breakpoint-containing windows. 70th percentile = breakpoints sit above-average in recombination rate.

statistics.name: fisher_one_sided_p
statistics.note: p = 0.089 — underpowered with 16 bins. The OR magnitude is the main signal; p reflects the small N of bins, not effect strength.

statistics.name: cohens_d_breakpoint_vs_matched_per_track
statistics.note: Per-track Cohen's d. Mirrors the recombination hotspot biophysical signature: high deformability, high GC, low curvature.

inference.justification: Two conjuncts: (1) Fisher OR > 2.0 establishes substantive enrichment of breakpoints in top-recombination 1 Mb windows; (2) median percentile > 0.6 establishes the OR is not just from a few extreme outliers but a shifted distribution. The Fisher p (0.089) is deliberately NOT in the conjunction — the test is underpowered at 16 bins; including p < 0.05 would falsify the moderate-positive registration the analysis intends. The per-track Cohen's d statistics are reported but not in the inference; they are the mechanistic story, not the falsifier.
inference.failure_mode: If s_fisher_or fell below 2.0 OR median percentile fell below 0.6, the shared-vulnerability framing would dissolve and translocation breakpoints would look distinct from meiotic hotspot biophysics.

conclusion.assertion: Cancer translocation breakpoints sit at the 70th percentile of crossover-rate-binned 1 Mb windows (Fisher OR = 2.34 enrichment in top-quartile recombination windows, p = 0.089 underpowered with 16 bins), and their biophysical signature (deformability d=+0.99, GC d=+0.99, curvature d=-0.95 vs matched controls) mirrors the recombination hotspot signature. The material channel creates a shared vulnerability surface for meiotic crossover and pathological chromosomal rearrangement.

scope.scale_note: 1 Mb windowed enrichment + per-breakpoint matched-control biophysics. Native-resolution breakpoint analysis is queued.

confidence.note: Underpowered at 16 bins for Fisher; effect-size-driven moderate-positive registration.

external_assumptions.statement: The PolymerGenomicsAPI breakpoints layer is curated from defensible cancer translocation panels (MITELMAN, COSMIC); coverage is uneven across cancer types but adequate for the 1 Mb windowed enrichment.

Matched controls (5 per breakpoint, chromosome-matched) defensibly bound the regional baseline biophysics; chromosome-and-distance matching would be stricter but was not used in the original A4 analysis.
